data.title: Analyst: Arthur Hayes uses the "front foot milk, back foot sell" strategy to sell ETHFI
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Arthur Hayes sold 1.216 million ETHFI using a "buy first, sell last" strategy. ETHFI was listed on Upbit today, and BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes sold 1.216 million ETHFI (US$1.89 million) 30 minutes ago: 136,000 ETHFI were sold through Galaxy Digital for 221,000 USDC, and 1.08 million ETHFI were transferred to Binance via Flowdesk.

data.title: MiCA under pressure as national regulators challenge passporting
data.description:
The EU’s landmark crypto law was meant to unify the market with a single license. Less than a year in, diverging national approaches are raising fears of regulatory arbitrage and uncertainty.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/europe-mica-framework-faces-early-test-as-national-regulators-push-back-passporting?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Understanding the Risks of Second-Hand Binance Accounts
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, highlighting the dangers associated with using second-hand Binance accounts. This practice, common in gaming where players buy accounts with rare items, poses significant security and compliance risks in the crypto world. The article emphasizes the importance of creating and securing one's own account to avoid potential vulnerabilities.

The risks of second-hand Binance accounts are manifold. Firstly, these accounts may have compromised login details, such as usernames, passwords, or recovery phrases, due to previous owners' poor management or malicious activities. This leaves the account vulnerable from the outset. Additionally, devices linked to these accounts might be infected with malware, threatening not only the account but the user's entire digital ecosystem. There is also the possibility that the account was involved in criminal activities, which could implicate the new user in legal issues. Unauthorized transactions initiated by former owners pose a direct threat to financial security and identity protection. Moreover, using a second-hand account violates the Binance User Agreement, risking suspension or permanent banning, which could lead to asset loss.

A recent case highlighted the sale of a second-hand Binance account on social media, specifically Binance Square. The seller advertised it as "ready-to-use," with a portfolio of altcoins, priced at 90% of the portfolio's total value. While it appeared to be a bargain, the risks were substantial. The original owner might still have access to login details or linked devices, risking lockouts or asset theft for the buyer. Furthermore, these accounts often bypass identity verification, exposing new holders to compliance issues and legal consequences. What seems like a cheap shortcut can quickly become an expensive and dangerous mistake.

To stay safe, the article advises against purchasing or using second-hand accounts. Instead, users should create and secure their own Binance accounts, employing robust security measures. These include enabling two-factor authentication (2FA), using passkeys, setting up email verification, employing physical security keys, and utilizing device management. Withdrawal address whitelisting and data encryption are also recommended to protect sensitive information. Regularly reviewing account activity and reporting suspicious actions to Binance Support are crucial steps in maintaining security. By following these practices, users can build a personalized, layered defense to safeguard their accounts.

In conclusion, while second-hand accounts may seem appealing, they carry hidden dangers that can compromise both funds and identity. The safest approach is to create and secure one's own Binance account, implementing strong protections like customizable 2FA, passkeys, and withdrawal whitelisting. Security is an ongoing journey, and staying informed through Binance's Security Series can help users trade and explore Web3 with confidence.

data.title: Bitcoin Treasury Firm Empery Digital Signs $50 Million in New Loan Agreement to Repurchase Shares
data.description: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital announced that it has signed a new $50 million loan agreement. In addition, the company has increased its previous committed borrowing limit from $25 million to $35 million. The funds will be used to repurchase shares. As of September 18, 2025, the company has repurchased 3,522,233 shares of common stock at an average repurchase price of $7.35 per share. (Businesswire)

data.title: Whale Leverages 25x to Long 21,966 ETH Worth $99.5 Million
data.description: According to BlockBeats, blockchain monitoring service Lookonchain has reported that a significant investor, identified as '0x89Da,' recently executed a leveraged trade involving 21,966 ETH, valued at approximately $99.5 million. This trade was conducted with a 25x leverage nine hours ago. The investor has a history of 53 transactions, boasting a success rate of 81.13% and profits exceeding $2 million.

data.title: ZOOZ Power Secures Approval for $180 Million Bitcoin Treasury
data.description: According to BlockBeats, ZOOZ Power, a publicly traded company on the U.S. stock market, has announced that a special shareholders' meeting has approved a previously announced $180 million fundraising initiative. The company plans to allocate approximately 95% of the net proceeds from this fundraising to purchase and hold Bitcoin.

data.title: Mega Matrix, a US-listed company, purchased ENA for approximately $6 million.
data.description: Mega Matrix, a NYSE American-listed company, announced that it has spent approximately $6 million on ENA tokens, including $3 million in purchases executed over the past week. The company now holds 8.46 million ENA tokens at an average purchase price of $0.7165 (including all fees and transaction costs). (PRNewswire)

data.title: Gate US Launches Fiat Currency Purchase Service
data.description: According to the latest news on Gate US's official website, the platform has officially launched a fiat currency buy feature. This feature allows users to directly purchase digital assets with amounts ranging from $20 to $15,000, further improving the convenience and compliance of crypto market entry for both retail and institutional investors. Gate US currently holds 30 state licenses and operates in compliance with 40 US jurisdictions.

data.title: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes is suspected of selling ETHFI through OTC
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes is suspected of selling ETHFI through OTC. He transferred 136,600 ETHFI to Galaxy Digital and received 221,000 USDC between 20:14 and 20:16 (UTC+8) today. He also transferred 1.08 million ETHFI to the suspected Flowdesk address: 0xab…9532, worth approximately US$1.67 million.

data.title: Matrixport's associated wallet deposited 88,434 ETH to Binance 4 hours ago
data.description: According to Lookonchain, a wallet associated with Matrixport deposited 88,434 ETH, worth $402 million, to Binance four hours ago. Notably, the wallet first conducted a test transaction of 100 ETH (US$454,000) before making the large deposit.

data.title: Fed's Kashkari predicts two more rate cuts this year
data.description: Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari said he supports the Fed's decision to cut interest rates this week and has included two more rate cuts this year in his forecast. "I believe the risk of a significant rise in unemployment is sufficient to prompt the committee to act to support the labor market," Kashkari wrote in an article published on his Fed website on Friday. Fed officials cut interest rates by 25 basis points at their Wednesday meeting, the first cut since December, citing the need to support a more fragile job market. The Minneapolis Fed president does not vote on monetary policy this year but participates in Federal Open Market Committee discussions. He had previously projected that two cumulative rate cuts would be sufficient for 2025. The Fed will hold two more policy meetings this year, in October and December. Kashkari added that the Fed should not "presume a set path of a series of rate cuts." If the labor market appears more resilient than it currently appears or inflation rises, he would favor holding steady; he would even consider raising rates if economic conditions warrant. (Jinshi)

data.title: BNB Surpasses 990 USDT with a Narrowed 0.52% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Sep 19, 2025, 13:05 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 990 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 990.23999 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.52% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: A whale transferred 15.1 million ENA to Binance an hour ago, earning $5.61 million
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, 15.1 million ENA (US$10.09 million) entered Binance an hour ago, with whales making a profit of US$5.61 million (+125%). These 15.1 million ENA were transferred from Bybit to the on-chain hoard three months ago, at a value of US$4.48 million and a price of US$0.29.

data.title: Fed's Kashkari: Fed cannot accept 3% inflation
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Neel Kashkari said the Fed cannot accept a 3% inflation rate and is confident it can reduce inflation to 2%. The Fed's credibility will not be damaged by undermining its inflation target, and Congress widely recognizes the Fed's independence. (Jinshi)

data.title: Former SEC Chairman Gary Gensler: I have no regrets about strictly regulating the crypto industry during my tenure
data.description: In an interview with CNBC reporter Sara Eisen, former SEC Chairman Gary Gensler expressed no regrets about his strict regulation of the crypto industry during his tenure. He stated, "I'm very proud of our achievements. We have always worked hard to ensure investor rights." Regarding his successor, current SEC Chairman Paul Atkins, Gary Gensler seemed unimpressed, insisting that, aside from Bitcoin, most cryptocurrencies in the crypto industry are still being hyped, divorced from fundamentals, and highly speculative. (cryptoinamerica)

data.title: Grayscale Launches Grayscale Crypto 5 ETF - CoinDesk
data.description: According to official news, Grayscale announced the launch of the Grayscale CoinDesk Crypto 5 ETF, which aims to track the five most mature (largest and most liquid) cryptocurrencies: Bitcoin, Ethereum, SOL, XRP and ADA.

data.title: 2 newly created wallets withdrew 851 BTC from the exchange
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, two newly created wallets withdrew 851 BTC worth $99 million from exchanges, indicating that whales are still accumulating Bitcoin.

data.title: Bank of Canada: Stablecoins are gradually becoming mainstream, and a unified federal regulatory framework is needed
data.description: Ron Morrow, Deputy Governor of the Bank of Canada, stated that stablecoins have gradually become mainstream in cross-border payments and daily transactions, and Canada needs to establish a unified federal regulatory framework to avoid falling behind the United States, Britain and other countries in payment modernization. He pointed out that the current provincial regulatory fragmentation is serious and called for the acceleration of the formulation of regulations to ensure the security of the financial system. Currently, the cost of cross-border remittances in Canada is as high as 5-10%, but stablecoins can reduce it to less than 1%. (Decrypt)

data.title: Trader ZetaCentauri made $3.648 million in profit in one month, with six consecutive profitable trades
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684 xtpa), contract trader ZetaCentauri has seen six consecutive profitable trades over the past month, totaling $3.648 million. He trades exclusively in SOL, ETH, and WLFI, bringing his account's total profit to $7.306 million. His trading style is relatively aggressive, with current holdings exceeding $6 million in WLFI, and his other holdings in ETH and SOL exceeding $10 million, all of which are currently long.
